$comment: Full 9-field catalog for Account Takeover (previously a null code-review catalog).

patterns.what_to_look_for: Password-reset token weak, guessable, long-lived, not single-use, or not bound to the account.
source_indicators: reset token from a weak RNG / sequential / timestamp
token not tied to the user id
blackbox_indicators: token for one account works for another
false_positive_checks: cryptographically-random single-use token bound to the account with short TTL
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Request a reset, confirm token entropy/single-use/binding properties against the account.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Critical; ATO via reset is a full compromise — describe the exact missing property.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Email/phone change or 2FA disable without re-authentication or confirmation to the OLD contact.
source_indicators: sensitive identity change path with no current-password / step-up check
no notification to the prior contact
blackbox_indicators: identity changed using only a session, no re-auth
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Attempt an email/2FA change with only a valid session; confirm no step-up required.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Chains with session theft into full ATO; note the missing step-up.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Auth response oracle / inconsistent error enabling user enumeration + credential stuffing at scale.
source_indicators: distinct messages/timing for unknown-user vs bad-password
false_positive_checks: uniform response + global rate limiting present
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Probe login with known vs unknown users; measure response/timing differences and throttling.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Medium-High depending on lockout; pairs with credential stuffing.
